rationale: Lens (1) VoteDecision lockstep: the twin's unresolved leg uses the IDENTICAL r.UnresolvedVerdicts() predicate at the IDENTICAL position (between REJECT/hard_block and threshold), returning VoteBlock. No new divergence with PanelGateDecision on the vote portion. (2) Message superset: leg 9.5 is NOT a contiguous byte-superset of leg 10 (it swaps '— threshold is' for '(threshold is' and inserts the slots clause), but the operative invariant — every substring pre-existing sub-threshold Block fixtures assert survives — holds: 'X/N APPROVE', 'threshold is T/N', 'consolidated-round-N.md', fence are all present. findings: LOCKSTEP CONFIRMED: gate.go leg 9.5 and tally.go VoteDecision both call the same UnresolvedVerdicts() (len>0 -> Block/VoteBlock), both positioned after REJECT/hard_block and before threshold. Leg 9.5 introduces no case where the vote-only twin can disagree with PanelGateDecision on the vote portion.

SUPERSET (nuance, non-blocking): the code comment / BRIEF say 'byte-superset', but leg 9.5's message is a substring-SET superset, not a contiguous byte superset — it renders 'APPROVE (threshold is' where leg 10 renders 'APPROVE — threshold is'. Any fixture asserting the em-dash phrase 'APPROVE — threshold' would break; none does. All actual assertions use disjoint substrings ('X/N APPROVE', 'threshold is T/N', consolidated name, fence) that ARE preserved. Wording could be tightened to 'substring-set superset' but no functional defect.

ALL LEGS BYTE-PRESERVED: git show 4dfebbcb -- gate.go/tally.go shows pure insertions; every other leg (abandoned/round-mismatch/missing-ref/transient/stale/dirty/incomplete/REJECT/threshold) is untouched.

TWIN-SIDE SUPERSET verified too: votedecision_test 'sub-threshold 4/6' (line 89) uses makeVerdicts() empty-string verdicts which are now 'unresolved', so it routes through the twin's unresolved leg — yet its 'threshold is 5/6' assertion survives because the twin message also carries 'threshold is T/N'. Green.
NO FALSE BLOCK: all-APPROVE companions (votedecision_test line 81 -> VotePass; panel_decision_test 3/3 line 222 -> Allow) confirm a clean panel still passes.
